_meta.review_warning: AI-drafted v0. DO NOT USE IN PRODUCTION until reviewed by a qualified Vic WHS practitioner and approved. Pool plumbing must be installed by a licensed plumber where the licensed scope applies (suction, return, balance tank, backwash to sewer).

activity_name: Pool plumbing rough-in (suction, return, vacuum, backwash, balance tank)

hazards.title: Fall into pool excavation while running pipe
hazards.risk: Plumber working at edge or in excavation during rough-in

controls.title: Edge protection in place; ladder access only
No solo work in excavation; partner above ground

hazards.title: PVC primer / cement solvent exposure
hazards.risk: Inhalation / skin contact with PVC priming fluid and cement

controls.title: SDS for all primers / cements on site

hazards.title: Manual handling — pipe and fittings
hazards.risk: Awkward lifts of long pipe and balance tanks

controls.title: Two-person lift for balance tanks and long pipe runs

tasks: Pre-start: confirm licensed plumber on site; confirm scope and drawings.
Lay out pipe runs per drawings: suction(s), return(s), main drain (if applicable), vacuum, backwash.
Trench / chase as required; coordinate with shell penetrations.
Cut, prime, and solvent-cement joints per AS/NZS 3500.
Pressure-test all lines to spec (typically 350 kPa for 24 hours); record test in Hub.
Connect balance tank with overflow to legal point of discharge.
Backwash line to sewer via approved trade-waste connection.
As-built sketch updated and uploaded to Hub.
Plumber sign-off in Hub before any cover-up / pour.
